label: call events
desc: OpenPhone sends call and voicemail events (caller/callee, timestamps, recording link, metadata) into Zapier so the pipeline starts automatically.

label: webhook POST
desc: Zapier POSTs the normalized event payload into the ingestion endpoint, including call IDs and recording URL, so it can be validated and stored.

label: Nova-2 ASR
desc: The transcribe step sends audio to Deepgram Nova-2 and receives the transcript (and timing metadata). This transcript becomes the primary text input for routing and extraction.

label: GPT-4o-mini
desc: segment-llm asks GPT‑4o‑mini to propose topic boundaries so multi-topic calls can be split into independent spans.

label: embed-3-sm
desc: embed-facts converts project facts into vector embeddings for similarity search. This powers retrieval in context-assembly and guardrails.

label: embed-3-sm
desc: journal-embed vectorizes journal claims so later queries can find relevant prior statements and support dedup/conflict detection.

label: Haiku
desc: ai-router calls Haiku to assign each span to the correct project (or decide it needs review), using the assembled context and guardrails.

label: Haiku
desc: generate-summary calls Haiku to produce a human-readable call summary and structured follow-up items.

label: Haiku
desc: journal-extract calls Haiku to extract structured journal claims and open loops from an attributed span.

label: Sonnet 4.5
desc: journal-consolidate uses Sonnet to merge new claims into the project record, resolving duplicates and flagging contradictions.

label: Haiku
desc: striking-detect calls Haiku to score urgency/risks and write striking signals used for ops prioritization.

label: Haiku
desc: chain-detect uses Haiku to link related calls into threads so the system can reason over continuity across interactions.

label: Haiku
desc: loop-closure uses Haiku to mark open loops as closed when later evidence confirms completion.

label: OAuth search
desc: gmail-ctx uses OAuth to search and summarize relevant Gmail threads, then returns/caches that context for the pipeline.

label: push trigger
desc: A GitHub push triggers CI/CD workflows that test and deploy edge functions and schema changes.

label: JWT verify
desc: review-resolve verifies the user’s Supabase JWT so only authorized reviewers can apply corrections.

label: forward (blocking)
desc: Ingestion forwards the call payload to process-call and waits for a response. This is the synchronous entry that validates, deduplicates, and persists the call.

label: fire & forget
desc: After storing the call, process-call kicks off segmentation asynchronously so the webhook can return quickly; downstream failures are tracked separately.

label: get boundaries
desc: segment-call requests span boundaries from segment-llm so the transcript can be split into topic-focused chunks within size limits.

label: per span (blocking)
desc: For each span, segment-call blocks on context-assembly to build the context_package needed for accurate routing.

label: context_package
desc: context-assembly hands ai-router a complete context package (span text + candidates + retrieved facts/claims) so routing can be decided consistently.

label: email fetch (fail-open)
desc: context-assembly optionally fetches cached email context; if Gmail is unavailable it proceeds without email so routing doesn’t stall.

label: per span f&f
desc: Each span triggers striking-detect asynchronously to compute urgency/risk signals without blocking the routing path.

label: if assign, f&f
desc: When a span is assigned to a project, segment-call fires journal-extract in the background to extract claims and follow-ups; failures don’t block attribution.

label: after all spans
desc: After all spans are processed, segment-call triggers generate-summary once to produce the call-level summary and task list (fire-and-forget).

label: belt & suspenders
desc: As a backup, ai-router also triggers journal-extract when it assigns a project, ensuring claim extraction still happens even if an earlier hook was skipped.

label: if claims>0
desc: If extraction produced claims, journal-consolidate runs to merge them into project state and detect conflicts.

label: queue if review
desc: When routing is uncertain or blocked by guardrails, ai-router enqueues an item for human review rather than guessing.

label: auth+id_guard
desc: Shared utilities enforce authentication, input normalization, and idempotency rules so process-call can safely accept retried webhooks.

label: llm_json
desc: Shared helpers standardize prompt/response JSON handling and schema validation so segmentation outputs stay structured and reliable.

label: auth+junk
desc: Shared code applies the same auth checks and low-signal/junk-call filtering so routing doesn’t waste compute or create review pressure.

label: auth
desc: Shared auth wrappers ensure only internal callers can assemble context packages.

label: auth+llm_json
desc: Shared auth + JSON schema helpers make claim extraction safer and easier to validate.

label: auth+llm_json
desc: Shared auth + schema helpers enforce consistent summary/task formats.

label: auth
desc: Shared auth ensures only privileged operators can apply ground-truth corrections.

label: 8 guardrails
desc: Guardrails provide hard constraints and sanity checks that ai-router must satisfy before assigning a project.

label: 6 retrievers
desc: Retrievers supply ranked signals (aliases, affinity, facts, claims, geo, email) that context-assembly packages for routing.

label: phone modules
desc: Phone normalization modules map raw from/to fields into consistent owner vs other-party roles for contact resolution and attribution.

label: reseg
desc: Reseed tooling reruns segmentation to rebuild spans when logic changes, while respecting locks that protect human decisions.

label: reroute
desc: Reseed reruns context assembly to refresh candidates and retrieved facts for existing spans.

label: reroute
desc: Reseed reruns routing on existing spans to test new router versions/config without silently overriding human locks.

label: redetect
desc: Reseed re-scores striking signals for existing spans to keep urgency metrics consistent after model/prompt updates.

label: reextract
desc: Reseed re-extracts journal claims for spans to backfill new claim types or improved prompts.

label: shadow replay
desc: Shadow replay re-ingests historical calls through process-call in an isolated mode to test changes without impacting production truth.

label: upsert interactions+calls_raw
desc: process-call upserts the interactions row and stores the normalized call in calls_raw, including contact resolution and candidate projects for downstream steps.

label: audio ref
desc: Stores a reference to the call recording location (usually a URL) so transcription can fetch audio later; the pipeline depends on this link staying valid.

label: idempotency+event_audit
desc: Writes idempotency keys and event_audit telemetry so duplicate webhooks are skipped and every run is traceable.

label: transcripts_comparison
desc: Persists transcript variants and quality metadata so you can compare ASR engines and track regressions over time.

label: upsert spans
desc: Writes conversation_spans (char ranges + segment text) for the interaction. Spans are the stable unit-of-work for routing and extraction.

label: upsert attributions
desc: Writes span_attributions as the system-of-record for which project each span belongs to, including lock state and confidence.

label: review_queue+alias_suggestions
desc: Creates review_queue items when attribution is uncertain and records potential alias suggestions discovered during routing.

label: via resolve_review_item
desc: When a human resolves a review item, review-resolve calls an atomic RPC that updates span_attributions (and related span records) in one transaction.

label: resolve+override_log
desc: Records the human decision and audit trail (override_log) so corrections are explainable and durable.

label: human_summary+scheduler
desc: Persists the call summary and materializes scheduler items/tasks for follow-up workflows.

label: event_audit
desc: Logs summary generation outcomes to event_audit for ops visibility and replay/debug.

label: striking_signals
desc: Stores per-span striking/urgency signals so dashboards and alerts can prioritize what needs attention.

label: claims+runs+loops
desc: Uses deduping RPCs to insert extracted claims, journal run metadata, and any open loops linked to the span/project.

label: relationships+conflicts
desc: Writes consolidated relationships and conflict records so contradictory claims can be triaged and resolved.

label: call_chains
desc: Stores call-to-call linkage so the system can reason over ongoing threads with a contact.

label: close loops
desc: Marks open loops as closed and appends closure evidence so the morning digest stops surfacing resolved items.

label: via apply_gt_correction
desc: Applies ground-truth corrections via RPC to lock the correct attribution permanently at the span level.

label: override_log
desc: Writes an audit entry for each GT change so evaluation and rollback analysis remain possible.

label: supersede+insert spans
desc: Supersedes old spans and inserts regenerated ones during reseed, preserving lineage and preventing mixed-span states.

label: cleanup+override_log
desc: Cleans up review state and records reseed actions so human workflows stay coherent.

label: suggested_aliases
desc: Writes candidate aliases discovered from text mining so humans can approve them and improve future routing.

label: approve/reject
desc: Applies human decisions on alias suggestions, promoting good aliases and rejecting noisy ones.

label: retire+expire
desc: Retires aliases for closed/inactive projects to reduce drift and false matches.

label: embeddings
desc: Stores embeddings for project facts in the world model, enabling semantic retrieval and time-aware context.

label: claim embeddings
desc: Stores vector embeddings for journal claims to support semantic search and better dedup/conflict detection.

label: gmail_context_cache
desc: Caches email context summaries with a TTL so repeated context-assembly runs don’t hammer Gmail.

label: span_place_mentions
desc: Persists detected place mentions from spans to support geo signals and later analysis.

label: project facts
desc: Guardrails reads authoritative project facts to block impossible assignments and validate extracted outputs.

label: fact embeddings
desc: Retrievers query fact embeddings to pull the most relevant project facts for the current span.

label: alias lookup
desc: Retrievers load active project aliases so context-assembly can match spoken names to projects.

label: fanout+affinity+geo
desc: Retrievers use precomputed affinity, fanout classification, and geo proximity to rank candidate projects.

label: claims+open_loops
desc: Retrievers pull recent claims and unresolved loops so routing and summaries reflect current project state.

label: contacts+projects
desc: process-call reads core contacts/projects to resolve the caller identity and limit candidates to eligible projects.

label: interactions+calls_raw
desc: generate-summary reads stored interactions, transcripts, and routing outputs to produce the final summary and tasks.

label: atomic update
desc: The resolve_review_item RPC updates span attribution atomically—either everything commits or nothing does.

label: atomic update
desc: The same transaction updates review_queue status and writes override logs so the audit trail always matches the attribution.

label: call
desc: review-resolve invokes resolve_review_item to apply a human decision safely and consistently.

label: call
desc: gt-apply calls apply_gt_correction for each labeled segment so corrections are applied under database-enforced invariants.

label: atomic update
desc: apply_gt_correction performs an atomic attribution update and enforces monotonic locks so later pipeline runs can’t override GT.

label: dedup insert
desc: journal-extract calls insert_journal_claims_dedup so retries don’t create duplicate claims and reruns are safe.

label: 4 RPCs
desc: process-call uses a small set of contact/alias RPCs for phone lookup, transcript scanning, and candidate expansion—keeping matching logic centralized and auditable.

label: enforces on UPDATE
desc: A database trigger enforces monotonic attribution locks on updates, preventing any code path from downgrading a human correction.

label: schema
desc: Migrations evolve schema and invariants; they must be applied before deploying code that depends on new tables/columns.

label: deploy EFs
desc: CI deploys edge functions and related config to Supabase so production matches what’s in GitHub.

label: batch
desc: Operational scripts launch batch reseeds to reprocess many interactions under controlled conditions.

label: batch
desc: Batch scripts apply ground-truth corrections at scale for evaluation and model iteration.

label: batch
desc: Batch shadow replays run historical traffic through the pipeline in a non-impacting mode to measure changes.

label: test all 13
desc: Smoke tests simulate inbound webhooks across pipeline endpoints to confirm auth and basic health after deploy.

label: preflight
desc: Claim guard enforces operator context and safety checks before any write-mode script can run.

label: unit tests
desc: Unit/regression tests validate guardrail behavior so routing constraints don’t drift as prompts and data evolve.
